title: Chewy Smoky Middle Eastern Lamb Skewers
description: Savory, chewy lamb skewers infused with a smoky bhuna spice blend, finished with a cool dollop of crème fraîche and fresh mint.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne, salt, pepper, tomato paste, honey, yogurt, and lemon juice to make a smooth bhuna‑style marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss until each piece is well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to penetrate.
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes before threading. Thread marinated lamb onto skewers, leaving a little space between pieces for even cooking.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 3‑4 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until the lamb reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) and develops a slightly charred, smoky crust.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes to retain juices.
Divide the skewers onto plates, then spoon a dollop of crème fraîche over each skewer and sprinkle with chopped mint.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side for an extra citrus kick.
